title: Conditionals should start on new lines
description: Conditionals should start on new linesAnalyze your codeconsistency-formattedmaintainabilityCode SmellsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?More InfoPlacing anifstatement on the same line as the closing}from a precedingif,else, orelse ifblock can lead to confusion and potential errors. It may indicate a missingelsestatement or create ambiguity for maintainers who might fail to understand that the two statements are unconnected.The following code snippet is confusing:if (condition1) { // ... } if (condition2) { // Noncompliant //..
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

detection_conditions: If statement appears immediately after closing brace } on same line
Not part of an else if chain
triggering_conditions: Closing brace } followed by 'if' keyword on same line
Multiple if statements chained on same line after blocks
Pattern } if detected without else keyword
exclusion_conditions: If statement at start of line (no preceding })
Single if statement without preceding block
Closing brace followed by newline before if
never_trigger_on: Properly formatted if on new line
If statements separated by blank lines from previous blocks
checks.reasoning: This rule improves code readability by preventing if statements from appearing on the same line as a closing brace, which can create confusion about code flow. The check_conditionals_on_same_line function detects the pattern '} if(' while excluding the valid 'else if' pattern. This helps maintainers understand that consecutive if statements are independent, not part of an if-else chain.

validation_examples.reason: Second if statement starts on same line as closing brace
If statement should start on new line after }
Properly formatted with if on new line
else if is an allowed pattern
